title: Lockstep Digital Twin Console
subtitle: Northwind Health / Unified Service Cloud
rules: Every accepted action is applied to the twin and the live adapter.
If the resulting state hashes diverge, execution halts and the twin is invalid.
Large evidence can remain referenced or derived until the action needs it.

actions.label: Promote the qualified lead
actions.clock: Discovery call was approved for promotion.

inputs.name: Lead frame
inputs.note: The promoted CRM rows are materialized directly into the twin.

inputs.name: Transcript archive
inputs.note: The full transcript stays external until an operator drills into it.

inputs.name: Budget confidence
inputs.note: Commercial confidence is recomputed instead of copied raw.

actions.note: Both systems promoted the lead into the same opportunity state.

actions.label: Publish the commercial offer
actions.clock: Pricing review and scope approval both cleared.

inputs.name: Proposal frame
inputs.note: The approved proposal state is small enough to stay embedded.

inputs.name: Pricing workbook
inputs.note: The detailed pricing workbook is referenced instead of copied into the frame.

inputs.name: Margin outlook
inputs.note: Margin is a derived summary, not a copied spreadsheet.

actions.note: Quote publication stayed aligned across the twin and the live system.

actions.label: Open the migration incident
actions.clock: Telemetry reported a failed migration batch.

inputs.name: Customer frame
inputs.note: The customer and opportunity state are already materialized.

inputs.name: Failure telemetry
inputs.note: The raw logs are far too large to embed, so the frame carries a pointer.

inputs.name: Account health score
inputs.source: derive(health <- telemetry + onboarding tasks + sponsor activity)
inputs.note: Health is derived from linked evidence and current state.

actions.note: The incident opens cleanly, so the twin remains trustworthy.

actions.label: Resolve the migration incident
actions.clock: Recovery workflow says the service should return to stable.

inputs.name: Recovery frame
inputs.note: The twin expects a resolved case and a healthy account state.

inputs.name: Adoption telemetry
inputs.note: The live adapter still consults the external usage stream before clearing the case.

inputs.name: Renewal readiness
inputs.source: derive(readiness <- adoption telemetry + billing health + case history)
inputs.note: Renewal readiness is derived from evidence across multiple ledgers.

actions.note: The live system keeps the case in monitoring, so the twin fails the lockstep test.

actions.label: Open the renewal motion
actions.clock: Commercial follow-up would normally begin once service is healthy.

inputs.name: Renewal opportunity frame
inputs.note: The next commercial state would be materialized if the twin remained valid.

inputs.name: Executive review ledger
inputs.note: The supporting success evidence stays external until needed.

inputs.name: Expansion score
inputs.source: derive(score <- adoption + sponsor sentiment + billing health)
inputs.note: Expansion readiness is derived, not copied raw.

actions.note: This action remains blocked if drift is detected on the prior step.

correctionPolicy.title: Correction frame policy
correctionPolicy.summary: If drift is detected, the machine re-slushes the twin projection and the live adapter response into a correction frame so the next durable event starts from truth without losing intent.

forkDimensions.name: Fork liquid feed
forkDimensions.note: A fork can feed a different liquid data stream into the same action machine.

forkDimensions.name: Correction pressure
forkDimensions.source: derive(correction-pressure <- fork liquid feed + live adapter drift + twin intent)
forkDimensions.note: Alternative dimensions can shape the correction frame without discarding the original ledger.

backupPolicy.title: Fork backup and reimport
backupPolicy.summary: Forks can export this action stream, edit the JSON locally, and reimport it without losing the correction-frame logic.
